title: Packages, Repositories, Dependencies, and Software Provenance · Transfer Runbook
summary: Install or update a required tool while preserving repository trust, version evidence, rollback options, and the boundary between system and project dependencies. The scenario, data, constraints, and failure modes differ from the workshop. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of a distribution-aware package change record with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence d…
objectives: Distinguish repositories, metadata, packages, dependencies, installed files, scripts, and transaction history.
Query package origin, version, architecture, files, dependencies, reverse dependencies, and candidate changes before mutation.
Evaluate repository ownership, signing keys, transport, scope, pinning, and update policy before adding a source.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# linux-basics cumulative operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic stateful terminal simulator.
# Record hypothesis, command, expected signal, observation, conclusion, and recovery.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model

steps.title: Extend the cumulative artifact

steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Package Rollback into observable performance evidence.

stimulus.title: Package Rollback failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: required

options.text: Keep the plausible output and hide the assumption to avoid confusing the stakeholder.

Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Package Rollback condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.
